name: Deadlock Resolution
text: A 'Deadlock' shall be deemed to occur if the Board of Directors or the Shareholders fail to reach a decision on a Reserved Matter within thirty (30) days of the matter being first tabled ('Deadlock Period'). Upon the occurrence of a Deadlock, the following escalation procedure shall apply: (a) within seven (7) days of the expiry of the Deadlock Period, senior representatives of each Party shall meet and attempt in good faith to resolve the Deadlock; (b) if the Deadlock remains unresolved after a further thirty (30) days, either Party may serve a written notice ('Deadlock Notice') on the other Party; (c) upon receipt of a Deadlock Notice, either Party may elect to purchase the other Party's entire shareholding at fair market value, as determined by an independent valuer mutually appointed by the Parties or, failing such appointment within fifteen (15) days, by the President of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India; and (d) if neither Party elects to buy out the other within thirty (30) days of the Deadlock Notice, the Parties shall jointly procure a sale of the Company to a third party.

legal_basis.note: Winding up — just and equitable ground

invalid_if: Valuation mechanism for buyout is absent
Deadlock trigger events are not defined
title: Deadlock Resolution
dispute_hotspots: Deadlock mechanism not in Articles — SHA-only deadlock provision may not bind third party shareholders
Texas shoot-out — party with deeper pockets can force out weaker partner at low valuation
S.271 just and equitable winding up — court may wind up deadlocked JV company
